The Ascensus provision includes additional options regarding retirement status, but the core requirement for five-percent owners aligns closely.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision allows for an election to defer distributions until retirement, which is not explicitly mentioned in the Relius provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision references Treasury Regulations that may not be included in the Relius text.

relius_provision_text: "Limitation Year" means the "determination period" used to determine Compensation. However, the Employer may elect a different Limitation Year in Appendix A to the Adoption Agreement (Special Effective Dates and Other Permitted Elections). All qualified plans maintained by the Employer must use the same Limitation Year. Furthermore, unless there is a change to a new Limitation Year, the Limitation Year will be a twelve (12) consecutive month period. In the case of an initial Limitation Year, the Limitation Year will be the twelve (12) consecutive month period ending on the last day of the period specified in the Adoption Agreement. If the Limitation Year is amended to a different twelve (12) consecutive month period, then the new "Limitation Year" must begin on a date within the "Limitation Year" in which the amendment is made. The Limitation Year may only be changed by a Plan amendment. Furthermore, if the Plan is terminated effective as of a date other than the last day of the Plan's Limitation Year, then the Plan is treated as if the Plan had been amended to change its Limitation Year. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
Means the Plan Year, unless the Adopting Employer has selected another 12-consecutive month period in the Adoption Agreement. All qualified plans maintained by the Employer must use the same Limitation Year. If the Limitation Year is amended to a different 12-consecutive month period, the new Limitation Year must begin on a date within the Limitation Year in which the amendment is made.

If a Plan is terminated effective as of a date other than the last day of the Plan’s Limitation Year, the Plan is treated as if the Plan was amended to change its Limitation Year. As a result of this deemed amendment, the Code section 415(c)(1)(A) dollar limit must be prorated under the short Limitation Year rules. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define the 'Limitation Year' in a similar manner, indicating it is a 12-consecutive month period that can be selected in the Adoption Agreement. They also stipulate that all qualified plans maintained by the Employer must use the same Limitation Year and address the treatment of the plan upon termination. The main difference is the terminology used ('Limitation Year' vs. 'Plan Year'), but they functionally govern the same requirement.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision uses 'Plan Year' as the default term, while Relius uses 'Limitation Year'. Additionally, Ascensus explicitly mentions proration of the Code section 415(c)(1)(A) dollar limit, which is not mentioned in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: "Military Differential Pay" means any differential wage payments made to an individual that represents an amount which, when added to the individual's military pay, approximates the amount of compensation that was paid to the individual while working for the Employer. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, an individual receiving a differential wage payment, as defined by Code §3401(h)(2), is treated as an Employee of the Employer making the payment. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a payment defined in Code section 3401(h)(2) that is made by the Employer to an individual performing service in the uniformed services. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address differential wage payments related to military service, but the Relius provision includes a definition that specifies the payment's relationship to the individual's prior compensation and treats the individual as an Employee, while the Ascensus provision is more general and does not include this context.
variance_notes: The Relius provision provides a more detailed definition and context for the differential pay, including its calculation and treatment of the individual as an Employee, which is not present in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "Nonselective Contribution" means the Employer's contributions to the Plan other than Elective Deferrals, any Qualified Nonselective Contributions and any Qualified Matching Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions described in Code section 401(k)(12)(B)(iii) and made pursuant to the Safe Harbor CODA formula elected by the Employer in the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions to retirement plans, specifically matching contributions. However, the Relius provision focuses on nonselective contributions and includes specific requirements for traditional matching contributions, while the Ascensus provision describes enhanced matching contributions under a safe harbor formula. The options available in each are not directly comparable, as they pertain to different types of matching contributions.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes traditional matching contributions and nonselective contributions, while the Ascensus provision focuses on enhanced matching contributions under a safe harbor. The eligibility requirements and specific matching formulas differ significantly.

relius_provision_text: "Non-Key Employee" means any Employee or Former Employee (and such Employee's or Former Employee's Beneficiaries) who is not a Key Employee. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a Plan determined to be a Top-Heavy Plan for any Plan Year pursuant to Plan Section 7.19. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address top-heavy plan requirements, but they differ in specificity and structure. The Relius provision explicitly defines 'Non-Key Employee' and outlines specific elections related to minimum allocations for Non-Key and Key Employees, while the Ascensus provision is more general and focuses on top-heavy contributions without detailing the distinction between Key and Non-Key Employees.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific definitions and options for both Key and Non-Key Employees, while the Ascensus provision lacks this distinction and focuses on general top-heavy contributions and minimum allocations.

relius_provision_text: "Normal Retirement Age" means the age elected in the Adoption Agreement at which time a Participant's Account shall be nonforfeitable (if the Participant is employed by the Employer on or after that date). For money purchase pension plans, if the Employer enforces a mandatory retirement age, then the Normal Retirement Age is the lesser of that mandatory age or the age specified in the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means the age specified in the Adoption Agreement. If the Employer enforces a mandatory retirement age, the Normal Retirement Age is the lesser of that mandatory age or the age specified in the Adoption Agreement. If no age is specified in the Adoption Agreement, the Normal Retirement Age will be age 59½ if the Plan is a profit sharing plan or age 62 if the Plan is a money purchase pension plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define 'Normal Retirement Age' in relation to the Adoption Agreement and a mandatory retirement age, indicating that they govern the same plan feature. They also provide similar options for determining the Normal Retirement Age, although the Ascensus provision includes default ages if none are specified.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision specifies default ages (59½ for profit sharing plans and 62 for money purchase pension plans) if no age is elected, which is not mentioned in the Relius provision. This adds a layer of specificity in the Ascensus document.

relius_provision_text: "1-Year Break in Service" means, if the Hour of Service method is used, the applicable computation period that is used to determine a Year of Service during which an Employee or Former Employee has not completed more than 500 Hours of Service. However, if the Employer selected, in the Service Crediting Method Section of the Adoption Agreement, to define a Year of Service as less than 1,000 Hours of Service, then the 500 Hours of Service in this definition of 1-Year Break in Service shall be proportionately reduced. Further, solely for the purpose of determining whether an Employee has incurred a 1-Year Break in Service, Hours of Service shall be credited for the computation period in which the absence from work begins, only if credit therefore is necessary to prevent the Employee from incurring a 1-Year Break in Service, or in any other case, in the immediately following computation period. The Hours of Service credited for a "maternity or paternity leave of absence" shall be those which would normally have been credited but for such absence, or, in any case in which the Administrator is unable to determine such hours normally credited, eight (8) Hours of Service per day. The total Hours of Service required to be credited for a "maternity or paternity leave of absence" shall not exceed the number of Hours of Service needed to prevent the Employee from incurring a 1-Year Break in Service. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
Means Special Rules Where Elapsed Time Method is Being Used – If elected by the Adopting Employer in the Adoption Agreement, the Elapsed Time method of determining service will apply. When this definition applies, for purposes of determining an Employee's initial or continued eligibility to participate in the Plan or the Vested interest in the Participant's Individual Account balance derived from Employer Contributions, an Employee will receive credit for all Periods of Service. An Employee will also receive credit for any Period of Service of less than 12 consecutive months if a Break in Service occurs.

Fractional periods of a year will be expressed in terms of months or days.

The definition of Break in Service in this Elapsed Time definition will replace the definitions of Break in Eligibility Service and Break in Vesting Service found in the Definitions section of the Plan.

Break in Service is a Period of Service of at least 12 consecutive months.

In the case of an individual who is absent from work for maternity or paternity reasons, the 12-consecutive month period beginning on the first anniversary of the first date of such absence will not constitute a Break in Service. For purposes of this Elapsed Time definition, an absence from work for maternity or paternity reasons means 1) by reason of the pregnancy of the individual, 2) by reason of the birth of a child of the individual, 3) by reason of the placement of a child with the individual in connection with the adoption of such child by such individual, or 4) for purposes of caring for such child for a period beginning immediately following such birth or placement.

Each Qualifying Participant will share in Employer Contributions for the Periods of Service beginning on the date the Employee commences participation under the Plan and ending on the first day of a Period of Severance or the date on which such Employee is no longer a member of an eligible class of Employees.

If the Employer is a member of an affiliated service group (under Code section 414(m)), a controlled group of corporations (under Code section 414(b)), a group of trades or businesses under common control (under Code section 414(2), or any other entity required to be aggregated with the Employer pursuant to Code section 414(o), service will be credited for any employment for any period of time for any other member of such group. Service will also be credited for any individual required to be credited under Code section 414(n) or Code section 414(o) to be considered an Employee of any Employer aggregated under Code sections 414(b), (c), or (m). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address breaks in service related to maternity or paternity leave, but they use different methods for determining service (Hours of Service vs. Elapsed Time) and have different definitions of what constitutes a Break in Service. The Relius provision focuses on Hours of Service and specific thresholds, while the Ascensus provision emphasizes Periods of Service and includes broader criteria for maternity/paternity leave.
variance_notes: Key differences include the method of service calculation (Hours of Service vs. Elapsed Time), the definition of Break in Service (1-Year Break vs. 12 consecutive months), and the treatment of maternity/paternity leave, which is more detailed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "Participating Employer" means an Employer which, with the consent of the "lead Employer" adopts the Plan pursuant to Section 11.1 or Article XIV. In addition, unless the context means otherwise, the term "Employer" shall include any Participating Employer which shall adopt this Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Plan is a money purchase pension plan, Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, but they govern different types of contributions and have different contexts. The Relius provision specifically addresses Participating Employers and includes various types of contributions, while the Ascensus provision focuses solely on money purchase pension plan contributions and does not mention Participating Employers.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes a broader definition of Employer contributions and mentions both Profit Sharing and Money Purchase Pension Plan contributions, while the Ascensus provision is limited to Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions and does not address the concept of Participating Employers.

relius_provision_text: "Period of Severance" means a continuous period of time during which an Employee is not employed by the Employer. Such period begins on the date the Employee retires, quits or is discharged, or if earlier, the twelve (12) consecutive month anniversary of the date on which the Employee was otherwise first absent from service. In the case of an individual who is absent from work for "maternity or paternity" reasons, the twelve (12) consecutive month period beginning on the first anniversary of the first day of such absence shall not constitute a one year Period of Severance. For purposes of this paragraph, an absence from work for "maternity or paternity" reasons means an absence from work due to pregnancy of the individual, by reason of the birth of a child of the individual, or by reason of the placement of a child with the individual in connection with the adoption of such child by such individual, or (d) for purposes of caring for such child for a period beginning immediately following such birth or placement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means the date the Employee ceases to be employed by the Employer, or if earlier, the 12-month anniversary of the date on which the Employee was otherwise first absent from service. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the concept of severance from service, but the Relius provision includes specific details about maternity and paternity absences that are not present in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Relius provision explicitly defines the 'Period of Severance' while the Ascensus provision only defines the 'Severance from Service Date'.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes a detailed explanation of maternity and paternity absences and their impact on the Period of Severance, which is not addressed in the Ascensus provision. This difference in scope affects the overall equivalence of the provisions.

relius_provision_text: "Plan" means this instrument hereinabove referred to as Ascensus, LLC Defined Contribution Pre-Approved Plan (Basic Plan Document 05 and the Adoption Agreement) as adopted by the Employer, including all amendments thereto and any appendix which is specifically permitted pursuant to the terms of the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means the pre-approved defined contribution plan adopted by the Employer that is intended to satisfy the requirements of Code section 401 and ERISA section 501. The Plan consists of this Basic Plan Document, the corresponding Adoption Agreement, the corresponding trust or custodial agreement, and any attachments or amendments, as completed and signed by the Adoption Employer, including any amendment provisions adopted prior to the Effective Date of the Plan that are not superseded by the provisions of this restated Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions describe the structure of a defined contribution plan, but they focus on different aspects. The SOURCE provision emphasizes the definition of the plan and includes specific references to employer nonelective contributions, while the TARGET provision outlines eligibility and allocation formulas without detailing employer contributions.
variance_notes: The SOURCE provision specifically mentions employer nonelective contributions and their types, whereas the TARGET provision does not address contributions directly but rather focuses on participant eligibility and allocation formulas. This indicates a difference in focus and scope, leading to a lack of semantic equivalence.

relius_provision_text: "Plan Year" means the Plan's accounting year as specified in the Adoption Agreement. Unless there is a Short Plan Year, the Plan Year will be a twelve-consecutive month period.
ascensus_provision_text: Means the 12-consecutive month period that coincides with the Adopting Employer’s tax year or such other 12-consecutive month period as is designated in the Adoption Agreement. Notwithstanding the preceding, a Plan Year may be a period less than 12 months, as defined in the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define the 'Plan Year' but differ in their flexibility regarding the duration of the Plan Year. The Relius provision specifies a standard 12-month period unless there is a Short Plan Year, while the Ascensus provision allows for a Plan Year to be less than 12 months as defined in the Adoption Agreement. This difference in flexibility indicates that they do not govern the same plan feature in an equivalent manner.
variance_notes: The Relius provision does not allow for a Plan Year shorter than 12 months unless specified as a Short Plan Year, whereas the Ascensus provision explicitly allows for a Plan Year of less than 12 months, which could lead to different plan outcomes.

relius_provision_text: "Pre-Retirement Survivor Annuity" means an immediate annuity for the life of a Participant's Spouse, the payments under which must be equal to the benefit which can be provided with the percentage, as specified in the Adoption Agreement, of the Participant's Vested interest in the Plan as of the date of death. If no election is made in the Adoption Agreement, the percentage shall be equal to fifty percent (50%). Furthermore, if less than one hundred percent (100%) of the Participant's Vested interest in the Plan is used to provide the Pre-Retirement Survivor Annuity, a proportionate share of each of the Participant's Accounts subject to the Pre-Retirement Survivor Annuity shall be used to provide the Pre-Retirement Survivor Annuity. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a survivor annuity for the life of the surviving Spouse of the Participant if the payments are not less than the amounts that would be payable as a survivor annuity under the Qualified Joint and Survivor Annuity under the Plan in accordance with Code section 417(c). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to survivor annuities for a participant's spouse, but they differ in specific requirements and definitions. The Relius provision specifies a percentage of the participant's vested interest, while the Ascensus provision refers to amounts payable under a Qualified Joint and Survivor Annuity without specifying a percentage. Additionally, the related adoption agreement elections differ significantly in focus and structure.
variance_notes: Relius specifies a percentage of vested interest for the survivor annuity, while Ascensus refers to amounts under a different annuity type. The adoption agreement elections are not directly comparable, as they pertain to different aspects of compensation and contributions.

relius_provision_text: "Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals" means a Participant's Elective Deferrals that are not includible in the Participant's gross income at the time deferred. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Elective Deferrals that are not included in a Contributing Participant's gross income at the time deferred. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define 'Pre-Tax Elective Deferrals' similarly and govern the same plan feature related to participant contributions. They also include options for matching contributions, although the specific details and structure of the options differ slightly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific matching contribution options and mentions safe harbor contributions, while the Ascensus provision focuses on eligibility requirements and QACA matching contributions. The Relius document has more detailed options for matching contributions compared to the Ascensus document.

relius_provision_text: "Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangement" (QACA) means an automatic contribution arrangement which meets the requirements of Section 12.9. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a Plan whereby Eligible Employees are automatically enrolled as Contributing Participants in the Plan and that requires Employer Contributions to the Plan as outlined in Plan Section 3.01(F) and Code sections 401(k)(13) and 401(m)(12) in order to be deemed to satisfy certain nondiscrimination testing requirements. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to Qualified Automatic Contribution Arrangements (QACA) and involve employer contributions, but they differ in specific requirements and the context of related adoption agreement elections. The Relius provision focuses on employer nonelective contributions, while the Ascensus provision emphasizes automatic enrollment and nondiscrimination testing requirements.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific references to employer nonelective contributions and their types, while the Ascensus provision outlines eligibility and contribution requirements without detailing the types of contributions. Additionally, the related adoption agreement elections differ significantly in focus.

relius_provision_text: "Qualified Matching Contribution" (QMAC) means any Employer matching contributions that are made pursuant to Sections 12.1(a)(2) (if elected in the Adoption Agreement), 12.5 and 12.7 or pursuant to any other Plan provision which provides for such contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions described in Code section 401(k)(12)(B)(iii) and made pursuant to the QACA formula elected by the Employer in the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer matching contributions, but they govern different types of matching contributions (QMAC vs. QACA Enhanced Matching Contributions). The options available in the Adoption Agreements also differ in structure and requirements, indicating they are not semantically equivalent.
variance_notes: The Relius provision focuses on Qualified Matching Contributions (QMAC) with specific sections and options for traditional matching contributions, while the Ascensus provision is centered around QACA Enhanced Matching Contributions, which have different eligibility and matching criteria.

relius_provision_text: "Qualified Nonelective Contribution" (QNEC) means the Employer's contributions to the Plan that are made pursuant to Sections 12.1(a)(4), 12.5 and 12.7 or pursuant to any other Plan provision which provides for such contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Employer Contributions (other than Matching Contributions, Qualified Matching Contributions, or Employer Profit Sharing Contributions) allocated to Participants' Individual Accounts that the Participants may not elect to receive in cash until distributed from the Plan; that are nonforfeitable when allocated to the Participants' Individual Accounts; and that are distributable only in accordance with the distribution provisions (other than hardships) that are applicable to Elective Deferrals. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions to retirement plans, but they define different types of contributions and have different eligibility and distribution requirements. The Relius provision focuses on Qualified Nonelective Contributions (QNEC) and includes specific matching contribution options, while the Ascensus provision defines QNEC in a broader context and emphasizes nonforfeitable contributions that cannot be received in cash until distribution.
variance_notes: Key differences include the specific definitions of contributions, eligibility requirements for participants, and the treatment of distributions. Relius includes specific matching contribution options, while Ascensus does not specify matching contributions in the same way.

relius_provision_text: "Roth Elective Deferrals" means a Participant's Elective Deferrals that are includible in the Participant's gross income at the time deferred and have been irrevocably designated as Roth Elective Deferrals at the time of the deferral. Roth Elective Deferrals shall be subject to the requirements of Sections 12.2(d) and 12.2(e) and shall, except as otherwise provided herein, be required to satisfy the nondiscrimination requirements of Regulation §1.401(k)-1(b), the provisions of which are incorporated herein by reference. A Participant's Roth Elective Deferrals will be maintained in a separate account containing only the Participant's Roth Elective Deferrals and gains and losses attributable to those Roth Elective Deferrals. In addition, the Administrator shall, to the extent necessary for proper reporting, separately account for any "in-plan Roth rollover contributions" (as defined in Section 12.11) that are transferred to a Participant's Roth Elective Deferral Account. The portion of a Participant's Account attributable to "in-plan Roth rollover contributions" is not subject to the distribution restrictions of Section 12.2(e). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Elective Deferrals that are includible in a Contributing Participant's gross income at the time deferred and have been irreversibly designated as Roth Elective Deferrals by the Contributing Participant in their deferral election. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define Roth Elective Deferrals in a similar manner, indicating that they are included in gross income at the time deferred and must be designated as such by the participant. They also both reference the need for separate accounting for these deferrals. The related adoption agreement elections in both documents provide options for matching contributions, although the specific options differ slightly in wording and structure.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision includes additional eligibility requirements (age and service) that are not explicitly mentioned in the Relius provision. Additionally, the Relius provision references specific sections for nondiscrimination requirements and in-plan Roth rollover contributions, which are not detailed in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "Salary Deferral Agreement" means an agreement between a Participant and the Employer, whereby the Participant elects to reduce Compensation by a specific dollar amount or percentage and the Employer agrees to contribute such amount into the 401(k) Plan. The election may be made electronically in a manner permitted by the Employer. A Salary Deferral Agreement may require that an election be stated in specific percentage increments (not greater than one percent (1%) increments) or in specific dollar amount increments (not greater than dollar increments that could exceed one percent (1%) of Compensation). A Salary Deferral Agreement may not be effective prior to the later of: (a) the date the Employee becomes a Participant; (b) the date the Participant agrees (including by automatic consent) to the Salary Deferral Agreement; or (c) the date the 401(k) plan is adopted by the Employer or applicable Participating Employer. A Salary Deferral Agreement is valid even though it is executed by an Employee before he or she actually becomes a Participant, so long as the Salary Deferral Agreement is not effective before the date the Employee becomes a Participant. A Salary Deferral Agreement may only apply to Compensation that becomes currently available to the Employee after the effective date of the Salary Deferral Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, for a specified group of Participants (either Highly Compensated Employees or non-Highly Compensated Employees) for a Plan Year, the average of the ratios (calculated separately for each Participant in such group) of 1) the amount of Employer Contributions actually paid to the Fund on behalf of such Participant for the Plan Year to 2) the Participant’s Compensation for such Plan Year. For purposes of calculating the ADP, Employer Contributions on behalf of any Participant will include: 1) any Elective Deferrals (other than Catch-up Contributions or Elective Deferrals subsequently distributed as a permissible withdrawal) made pursuant to the Participant’s salary deferral election or pursuant to automatic Elective Deferral enrollment, if applicable (including Excess Elective Deferrals of Highly Compensated Employees), but excluding a) Excess Elective Deferrals of Participants who are non-Highly Compensated Employees that arise solely from Elective Deferrals made under the Plan or plans of this Employer and b) Elective Deferrals that are taken into account in the Actual Deferral Percentage test (provided that the ADP test is satisfied both before and after exclusion of these Elective Deferrals); and 2) if elected by the Employer, Qualified Nonelective Contributions and/or Qualified Matching Contributions. For purposes of computing Actual Deferral Percentages, an Employee who would be a Participant but for the failure to make Elective Deferrals will be treated as a Participant on whose behalf no Elective Deferrals are made. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions and participant elections in a retirement plan context, but they govern different aspects. The Relius provision focuses on the Salary Deferral Agreement and the mechanics of participant elections for deferrals, while the Ascensus provision centers on the Actual Deferral Percentage (ADP) calculation and its implications for contributions. Although they are related to matching contributions, they do not govern the same feature directly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision specifies the conditions under which a Salary Deferral Agreement is effective and the mechanics of participant elections, while the Ascensus provision defines how to calculate the ADP and includes specific exclusions and inclusions for contributions. The options available in each are not directly comparable as they pertain to different aspects of plan administration.

relius_provision_text: "Self-Employed Individual" means an individual who has Earned Income for the taxable year from the trade or business for which the Plan is established, and, also, an individual who would have had Earned Income but for the fact that the trade or business had no net profits for the taxable year. A Self-Employed Individual shall be treated as an Employee.
ascensus_provision_text: Means an individual who has Earned Income for the taxable year from the trade or business for which the Plan is established, including an individual who would have earned income but for the fact that the trade or business had no net profits for the taxable year.
reasoning: Both provisions define a 'Self-Employed Individual' in a similar manner, focusing on the requirement of having Earned Income from the trade or business for which the Plan is established, and including individuals who would have earned income if not for the lack of net profits.
variance_notes: The primary difference is the phrasing; the Relius provision explicitly states that a Self-Employed Individual 'shall be treated as an Employee,' which is not mentioned in the Ascensus provision. However, this does not significantly alter the core definition of a Self-Employed Individual.

relius_provision_text: "Taxable Wage Base" means, with respect to any Plan Year, the contribution and benefit base under Section 230 of the Social Security Act at the beginning of such Plan Year. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, with respect to any taxable year, the contribution and benefit base in effect in Section 230 of the Social Security Act at the beginning of the Plan Year. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define the 'Taxable Wage Base' in relation to the Social Security Act, but they differ in the context of their related adoption agreement elections. The Relius provision includes specific options related to Employer Nonelective Contributions, while the Ascensus provision does not provide comparable options, focusing instead on participant eligibility and allocation formulas.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific references to Employer Nonelective Contributions and related questions, while the Ascensus provision lacks these details and instead includes unrelated eligibility and allocation options.

relius_provision_text: "Terminated Participant" means a person who has been a Participant, but whose employment has been terminated with the Employer (including an Affiliated Employer) or applicable Participating Employer, other than by death, Total and Permanent Disability or retirement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means that the employment status of an Employee ceases for any reason other than death. An Employee who does not return to work for the Employer on or before the expiration of an authorized leave of absence from such Employer will be deemed to have incurred a Termination of Employment when such leave ends. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the termination of employment and its implications for plan participation, but they define 'termination' differently. The Relius provision specifies that termination excludes death, total and permanent disability, or retirement, while the Ascensus provision only excludes death. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes specifics about leaves of absence, which are not mentioned in the Relius provision.
variance_notes: Key differences include the exclusion criteria for termination (Relius includes more exceptions) and the treatment of leaves of absence in the Ascensus provision.

relius_provision_text: "Top-Heavy Plan" means a plan described in Section 9.2(a). [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a Plan determined to be a Top-Heavy Plan for any Plan Year pursuant to Plan Section 7.19. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the same plan feature regarding top-heavy plans and outline minimum allocation requirements. They provide options for how these allocations apply to Key and Non-Key Employee Participants, indicating a similar structure and intent.
variance_notes: The source provision specifies the application of minimum allocation requirements only to Non-Key Employee Participants unless an option is selected, while the target provision includes a more explicit selection process for minimum allocations. Additionally, the section references differ (Section 9.2(a) vs. Section 7.19), but the core concept remains aligned.

relius_provision_text: "Top-Heavy Plan Year" means a Plan Year during which the Plan is a Top-Heavy Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a Plan determined to be a Top-Heavy Plan for any Plan Year pursuant to Plan Section 7.19. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions govern the same plan feature regarding top-heavy plans and minimum allocation requirements. They provide options for minimum allocations to both Key and Non-Key Employee Participants, although the specific wording and section references differ.
variance_notes: The Relius provision explicitly mentions the application of minimum allocation requirements only to Non-Key Employee Participants unless an option is selected, while the Ascensus provision allows for a selection regarding minimum allocation or benefit without specifying the default application. Additionally, the section references and specific wording differ, but the overall intent and structure are similar.

relius_provision_text: "Top-Paid Group" shall be determined pursuant to Code §414(q) and the Regulations thereunder and generally means the top twenty percent (20%) of Employees who performed services for the Employer during the applicable year, ranked according to the amount of 415 Compensation received from the Employer during such year. All Affiliated Employers shall be taken into account as a single employer, and Leased Employees shall be treated as Employees if required by Code §414(n) or (o). Employees who are nonresident aliens who received no earned income (within the meaning of Code §911(d)(2)) from the Employer constituting United States source income within the meaning of Code §861(a)(3) shall not be treated as Employees. Furthermore, for the purpose of determining the number of Employees in any year, the following additional Employees may be excluded, however, such Employees shall still be considered for the purpose of identifying the particular Employees in the Top-Paid Group: [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means any Employee who 1) was a five-percent owner at any time during the year or the preceding year, or 2) for the preceding year had Compensation from the Employer in excess of $80,000 and, if elected by the Adopting Employer in the Adoption Agreement, was in the top-paid group for the preceding year. The $80,000 amount is adjusted at the same time and in the same manner as under Code section 415(d), except that the base period is the calendar quarter ending September 30, 1996. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employee classifications for contribution purposes, but they define different groups: the Relius provision defines a 'Top-Paid Group' based on the top 20% of employees by compensation, while the Ascensus provision defines 'Highly Compensated Employee' based on ownership and a specific compensation threshold. The options/elections available in the related adoption agreements also differ significantly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision focuses on a percentage of employees based on compensation, while the Ascensus provision includes ownership criteria and a fixed compensation threshold. The related adoption agreement elections are not directly comparable, as they pertain to different eligibility and allocation criteria.

relius_provision_text: In addition, if ninety percent (90%) or more of the Employees of the Employer are covered under agreements the Secretary of Labor finds to be collective bargaining agreements between Employee representatives and the Employer, and the Plan covers only Employees who are not covered under such agreements, then Employees covered by such agreements shall be excluded from both the total number of active Employees as well as from the identification of particular Employees in the Top-Paid Group. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: i. Identify the Employees of the Employer who are not Highly Compensated Employees of such Employer who participate in the Plan, and determine the average allocation rate for such group of Employees. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employee contributions and eligibility for participation in the plan, but they focus on different aspects. The Relius provision specifically addresses the exclusion of employees covered by collective bargaining agreements from the Top-Paid Group, while the Ascensus provision discusses identifying non-Highly Compensated Employees and determining average allocation rates. They do not govern the same plan feature directly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is concerned with collective bargaining agreements and their impact on employee classification, whereas the Ascensus provision focuses on allocation rates for non-Highly Compensated Employees. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ significantly in focus.

relius_provision_text: "Total and Permanent Disability" means, unless otherwise specified in Appendix A to the Adoption Agreement (Special Effective Dates and Other Permitted Elections), the inability to engage in any substantial gainful activity by reason of any medically determinable physical or mental impairment that can be expected to result in death or which has lasted or can be expected to last for a continuous period of not less than twelve (12) months. The disability of a Participant shall be determined by a licensed physician. However, if the condition constitutes total disability under the federal Social Security Acts, the Administrator may rely upon such determination that the Participant is Totally and Permanently Disabled for the purposes of this Plan. The determination shall be applied uniformly to all Participants.
ascensus_provision_text: Unless the Adopting Employer has elected a different definition in the Adoption Agreement or as otherwise provided in the Plan, Disability means the inability to engage in any substantial, gainful activity by reason of any medically determinable physical or mental impairment that can be expected to result in death or that has lasted or can be expected to last for a continuous period of not less than 12 months. The permanence and degree of such impairment will be supported by medical evidence satisfactory to the Plan Administrator. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define 'Disability' in a similar manner, focusing on the inability to engage in substantial gainful activity due to a medically determinable impairment lasting at least 12 months. They also allow for a determination by a licensed physician and reference the possibility of relying on Social Security determinations. The slight differences in wording do not significantly alter the meaning or application.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision emphasizes that the permanence and degree of impairment must be supported by medical evidence satisfactory to the Plan Administrator, while the Relius provision does not explicitly mention this requirement. Additionally, the Relius provision includes a reference to Appendix A for potential variations, which is not present in the Ascensus text.

relius_provision_text: "Trustee" means any person or entity that has agreed to serve as Trustee pursuant to the terms of the Trust agreement, or any successors thereto. The Employer may designate Trustees by business position or title. In addition, unless the context means, or the Plan provides, otherwise, the term "Trustee" shall mean the Insurer if the Plan is fully insured. The Employer has no reliance on the IRS opinion letter with respect to the separate Trust agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, if applicable, an individual, individuals, or corporation appointed in a separate trust agreement by the Adopting Employer as Trustee or any duly appointed successor. A corporate Trustee must be a bank, trust company, broker, dealer, or clearing agency as defined in Labor Regulation section 2550.403(a)-1(b). In the event of any conflict between the terms of the Plan and the terms of the separate trust agreement, the terms of the Plan will control. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define the role of a Trustee in the context of a retirement plan, but they differ in specific language and implications regarding conflicts with trust agreements. The Relius provision emphasizes the Employer's designation and the role of the Insurer in fully insured plans, while the Ascensus provision specifies the qualifications for a corporate Trustee and states that the Plan terms will control in case of conflicts.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's mention of the Insurer in fully insured plans and the lack of a conflict resolution clause in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes specific qualifications for corporate Trustees, which are not present in the Relius text.

relius_provision_text: "Trust Fund" means, if the Plan is funded with a trust, the assets of the Plan and Trust as the same shall exist from time to time.
ascensus_provision_text: Means the Plan assets held by the Trustee (or Custodian, if applicable) for the Participants' exclusive benefit.
reasoning: Both provisions define the assets of the Plan, indicating that they are held in trust for the benefit of the participants. They govern the same plan feature regarding the management and purpose of the trust fund.
variance_notes: The Relius provision emphasizes the existence of the trust and its assets over time, while the Ascensus provision specifies the role of the Trustee or Custodian and the exclusive benefit of the participants. However, both ultimately refer to the same concept of plan assets held in trust.

relius_provision_text: "Vested" means the nonforfeitable portion of any Account maintained on behalf of a Participant. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means nonforfeitable, that is, an unconditional and legally enforceable claim against the Plan that is obtained by a Participant or the Participant's Beneficiary to that part of an immediate or deferred benefit under the Plan that arises from a Participant's Years of Vesting Service. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define 'vested' as the nonforfeitable portion of a participant's account, indicating that they govern the same plan feature related to vesting. The options available in both documents allow for 100% vesting under certain conditions, suggesting comparable outcomes.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision includes specific references to Years of Vesting Service and mentions direct in-plan Roth rollovers, which are not explicitly covered in the Relius provision. However, the core concept of vesting remains aligned.

relius_provision_text: "Year of Service" means the computation period of twelve (12) consecutive months, herein set forth, and during which an Employee has completed at least 1,000 Hours of Service (unless a lower number of Hours of Service is specified in the Adoption Agreement). For purposes of eligibility for participation, the initial computation period shall begin with the date on which the Employee first performs an Hour of Service (employment commencement date). Unless otherwise elected in the Adoption Agreement, the succeeding computation period shall begin on the anniversary of the Employee's employment commencement date. However, unless otherwise elected in the Adoption Agreement, if one (1) Year of Service or less is required as a condition of eligibility, then the computation period after the initial computation period shall shift to the current Plan Year which includes the [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
Means a 12-consecutive month period that coincides with an Eligibility Computation Period during which an Employee completes at least 1,000 Hours of Service (or such lesser number of Hours of Service specified in the Adoption Agreement), or such period specified in the Period of Service definition, if applicable. Employees are not credited with a Year of Eligibility Service until they complete the required number of Hours of Service and reach the end of the 12-consecutive month period, or are employed at the end of the Period of Service, if applicable.

The Plan may be amended to change the method of crediting service between the Hours of Service method of determining service and the Elapsed Time method provided each Employee with respect to whom the method of crediting service is changed is afforded the protection described in Treasury Regulation section 1.410(a)-7g and other applicable rules promulgated by the IRS. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define a 'Year of Service' or 'Year of Eligibility Service' based on a 12-consecutive month period during which an employee completes at least 1,000 Hours of Service. They also allow for a lower threshold of hours as specified in the Adoption Agreement. The provisions govern the same eligibility feature for participation in the retirement plan.
variance_notes: The Ascensus provision includes additional language regarding the potential for amending the method of crediting service, which is not explicitly mentioned in the Relius provision. However, this does not significantly alter the core eligibility requirement.

relius_provision_text: Allocation of gains or losses. As of each Valuation Date, all Participant Directed Accounts shall be charged or credited with the net earnings, gains, losses and expenses as well as any appreciation or depreciation in the market value using publicly listed fair market values when available or appropriate as follows: (1) to the extent the assets in a Participant Directed Account are accounted for as pooled assets or investments, the allocation of earnings, gains and losses of each Participant's Account shall be based upon the total amount of funds so invested in a manner proportionate to the Participant's share of such pooled investment; and (2) to the extent the assets in a Participant Directed Account are accounted for as segregated assets, the allocation of earnings, gains on and losses from such assets shall be made on a separate and distinct basis. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_title: Employer Contributions Invested in Employer Securities
ascensus_provision_text: In the case of the portion of an Individual Account attributable to Employer Contributions that are invested in employer securities, a Participant may elect to direct the Plan to divest any such securities and to reinvest an equivalent amount in other investments that meet the investment option requirements below. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to participant-directed investments and employer contributions, but they govern different aspects. The Relius provision focuses on the allocation of gains and losses in participant-directed accounts, while the Ascensus provision addresses the ability of participants to divest employer securities and reinvest in other options. They do not cover the same plan feature directly.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is about the allocation of investment performance in participant accounts, while the Ascensus provision allows for the divestment of employer securities, indicating a difference in focus and functionality.

relius_title: INTEGRATION IN MORE THAN ONE PLAN
relius_provision_text: If the Employer maintains qualified retirement plans that provide for permitted disparity (integration), the provisions of Section 4.3(b)(2) will apply. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Plan is a money purchase pension plan, Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions in retirement plans, but they address different aspects. The Relius provision discusses integration in multiple plans and permitted disparity, while the Ascensus provision specifically designates contributions in a money purchase pension plan context. The related adoption agreement elections also differ significantly in focus.
variance_notes: Relius focuses on integration and permitted disparity across multiple plans, while Ascensus specifies the treatment of contributions in a money purchase pension plan. The related elections in each document do not align in terms of content or purpose.

relius_title: TRANSFER OF ASSETS FROM TERMINATED EMPLOYER DEFINED BENEFIT PENSION PLAN
relius_provision_text: Annual additions. Notwithstanding anything in the Plan to the contrary, amounts in the "transferred suspension account" shall not be treated as "annual additions" pursuant to Section 4.4 until such amounts are released and allocated to Participants. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to contributions and annual additions, but they address different aspects of the plan. The Relius provision specifically discusses the treatment of transferred assets from a terminated defined benefit plan, while the Ascensus provision outlines the components that constitute annual additions without reference to transferred assets.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific language about 'transferred suspension account' and its treatment until amounts are allocated to participants, which is not addressed in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes a broader range of contribution types and does not mention any specific treatment of transferred assets.

relius_provision_text: All benefits provided to a Participant in this Plan shall be subject to the rights afforded to any Alternate Payee under a "qualified domestic relations order." Furthermore, unless otherwise elected in Appendix A to the Adoption Agreement (Special Effective Dates and Other Permitted Elections), a distribution to an Alternate Payee shall be permitted if such distribution is authorized by a "qualified domestic relations order," even if the affected Participant has not reached the "earliest retirement age." For the purposes of this Section, "qualified domestic relations order" and "earliest retirement age" shall have the meanings set forth under Code §414(p). A domestic relations order that otherwise satisfies the requirements for a "qualified domestic relations order" will not fail to be a "qualified domestic relations order" solely because the order is issued after, or revises, another domestic relations order or "qualified domestic relations order"; or (ii) solely because of the time at which the order is issued, including issuance after the Annuity Starting Date or after the Participant's death. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
A Domestic Relations Order will not be treated as failing to meet the requirements above solely because such order requires that payment of benefits be made to an Alternate Payee

1. on or after the date on which the Participant attains (or would have attained) the earliest retirement age as defined in Code section 414(p)(4)(B),

2. as if the Participant had retired on the date on which such payment is to begin under such order, and

3. in any form in which such benefits may be paid under the Plan to the Participant (other than in a Qualified Joint and Survivor Annuity) with respect to the Alternate Payee and their subsequent spouse. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the treatment of Domestic Relations Orders (DROs) and their implications for distributions to Alternate Payees. They both allow for distributions regardless of the Participant's retirement status, aligning with the requirements of Code §414(p).
variance_notes: The Relius provision explicitly mentions that a distribution is permitted even if the Participant has not reached the earliest retirement age, while the Ascensus provision specifies conditions under which payments can be made, including the requirement that they begin after the earliest retirement age. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes specific language about the form of payment, which is not detailed in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Whenever the Employer under the terms of the Plan is permitted or required to do or perform any act or matter or thing, it shall be done and performed by a person duly authorized by its legally constituted authority. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Plan is a money purchase pension plan, Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, but they govern different aspects of those contributions. The Relius provision focuses on the authority of the employer to act regarding contributions, while the Ascensus provision specifically addresses the designation of contributions in a money purchase pension plan context.
variance_notes: The Relius provision emphasizes the employer's authority and includes various types of contributions, whereas the Ascensus provision is limited to designating prevailing wage contributions within a money purchase pension plan. The related adoption agreement elections also differ significantly in focus.

relius_title: NAMED FIDUCIARIES AND ALLOCATION OF RESPONSIBILITY
relius_provision_text: The 'named Fiduciaries' of this Plan are (a) the Employer, (b) the Administrator, (c) the Trustee (if the Trustee has discretionary authority), and (d) any Investment Manager appointed hereunder. The Employer may, however, modify the provisions of this Plan with respect to the appointment and removal of named Fiduciaries. The named Fiduciaries shall have only those specific powers, duties, responsibilities, and obligations as are specifically given them under the Plan, trust agreement, or similar agreement (as applicable) including, but not limited to, any agreement allocating or delegating their responsibilities, the terms of which are incorporated herein by reference. In general, the Employer shall have the sole responsibility for making the contributions provided for under the Plan; and shall have the sole authority to appoint and remove the Trustee and the Administrator; to formulate the Plan's 'funding policy and method'; and to amend the elective provisions of the Adoption Agreement or terminate, in whole or in part, the Plan. The Administrator shall have the sole responsibility for the administration of the Plan, which responsibility is specifically described in the Plan. If the Trustee has discretionary authority, it shall have the sole responsibility of management of the assets held under the Trust, except those assets, the management of which has been assigned to an Investment Manager or Administrator, who shall be solely responsible for the management of the assets assigned to them and not of the assets of the Plan as a whole. Any person or group may serve in more than one Fiduciary capacity. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means a person who exercises any discretionary authority or control with respect to management of the Plan, renders investment advice as defined in ERISA section 3(21), or has any discretionary authority or responsibility regarding the administration of the Plan. The Employer and such other individuals either appointed by the Employer or deemed to be fiduciaries as a result of their actions shall serve as Fiduciaries under this Plan and fulfill the fiduciary responsibilities described in Part 4, Title I of ERISA including discharging their duties with respect to the Plan solely in the interest of the Participants and Beneficiaries and with the undivided loyalty and diligence under the circumstances prevailing at that prudent man acting in a like capacity and familiar with such matters would use in the conduct of an enterprise of a like character and with like aims. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address fiduciary responsibilities within a retirement plan context, but they focus on different aspects. The Relius provision details the named fiduciaries and their specific powers, while the Ascensus provision defines fiduciaries more generally and emphasizes their duties under ERISA. The Relius provision is more specific about the roles and responsibilities of named fiduciaries, whereas the Ascensus provision is broader and does not specify named fiduciaries.
variance_notes: Key differences include the Relius provision's focus on named fiduciaries and their specific powers, while the Ascensus provision provides a general definition of fiduciaries and their responsibilities under ERISA without naming specific roles.

relius_provision_text: Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, if, pursuant to an application for qualification is made by the time prescribed by law for filing the Employer's return for the taxable year in which the Plan or an amendment to the Plan is adopted, or such later date as the Secretary of Treasury may prescribe, the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service or the Commissioner's delegate should determine that the Plan does not initially qualify as a tax-exempt plan under Code §§ 401 and 501, and such determination is not contested, or if contested, is finally upheld, then if the Plan is a new plan, it shall be void ab initio and all amounts contributed to the Plan by the Employer, less expenses paid, shall be returned within one (1) year and the Plan shall terminate, and the Trustee (or Insurer) shall be discharged from all further obligations. If the disqualification relates to Plan amendment, such Plan will operate as if it had not been amended. If the Employer's Plan fails to attain or retain qualification, such Plan will no longer participate in this pre-approved plan and will be considered an individually designed plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_title: Return of the Employer Contribution to the Employer Under Special Circumstances
ascensus_provision_text: F. Return of the Employer Contribution to the Employer Under Special Circumstances – Any contribution made by the Employer because of a mistake of fact must be returned to the Employer within one year of the contribution. In the event that the Commissioner of Internal Revenue determines that the Plan is not initially qualified under the Code, any contributions made incident to that initial qualification by the Employer must be returned to the Employer within one year after the date the initial qualification is denied, but only if the application for qualification is made by the time prescribed by law for filing the Employer's return for the taxable year in which the Plan is adopted, or such later date as the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe. In the event that a contribution made by the Employer under this Plan is conditioned on deductibility and is not deductible under Code section 404, the contribution, to the extent of the amount disallowed, must be returned to the Employer within one year after the deduction is disallowed. If applicable, no contract will be purchased under the Plan unless such contract or a separate definite written agreement between the Employer and the insurer provides that no value under such contracts providing benefits under the Plan or credits determined by the insurer (on account of dividends, earnings, or other experience rating credits, or surrender or cancellation credits) with respect to such contracts may be paid or returned to the Employer or directed to or used for other than the exclusive benefit of the Participants or their Beneficiaries. However, any contribution made by the Employer because of a mistake of fact must be returned to the Employer within one year of the contribution. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions address the return of employer contributions under specific circumstances related to plan qualification, but they differ in the details of the conditions and the scope of application. The Relius provision is more comprehensive regarding disqualification and its implications, while the Ascensus provision focuses on mistakes of fact and deductibility issues.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes specific language about the plan being void ab initio and the implications of disqualification on plan amendments, which is not present in the Ascensus provision. Additionally, the Ascensus provision includes a clause about contributions conditioned on deductibility, which is not mentioned in the Relius provision.

relius_provision_text: Except as otherwise provided in the Plan, benefits under this Plan shall be paid, subject to Sections 6.11, 6.12 and 12.10, only upon death, Total and Permanent Disability, normal or early retirement, severance of employment, or termination of the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text:
A Domestic Relations Order will not be treated as failing to meet the requirements above solely because such order requires that payment of benefits be made to an Alternate Payee

1. on or after the date on which the Participant attains (or would have attained) the earliest retirement age as defined in Code section 414(p)(4)(B),

2. as if the Participant had retired on the date on which such payment is to begin under such order, and

3. in any form in which such benefits may be paid under the Plan to the Participant (other than in a Qualified Joint and Survivor Annuity) with respect to the Alternate Payee and their subsequent spouse. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: While both provisions relate to the payment of benefits under a retirement plan, they govern different aspects. The Relius provision focuses on the conditions under which benefits are paid (e.g., death, disability, retirement), whereas the Ascensus provision addresses the treatment of benefits in the context of Domestic Relations Orders. The available options in the adoption agreements also pertain to different aspects of compensation and deferral elections, making them not directly comparable.
variance_notes: The Relius provision is more focused on the triggering events for benefit payments, while the Ascensus provision deals with specific legal orders affecting benefit payments. The adoption agreement elections are also not aligned in purpose or structure.

relius_provision_text: If this Plan is funded solely with Contracts, then notwithstanding Sections 10.7 and 10.13, no Contract will be purchased under the Plan unless such Contract or a separate definite written agreement between the Employer and the Insurer provides that value under such Contract and the Benefits provided by the Insurer are to be allocated to Participants' Accounts under the Plan and distributed to Participants or Beneficiaries in accordance with the terms of the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Plan is a money purchase pension plan, Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, but they govern different types of plans and have different focuses. The Relius provision specifically addresses contracts and allocation of benefits under a plan funded solely with contracts, while the Ascensus provision pertains to money purchase pension plans and designates contributions. The options available in the adoption agreements also differ significantly.
variance_notes: Relius focuses on the allocation of benefits under contracts, while Ascensus specifies the designation of contributions in a money purchase pension plan context. The related adoption agreement elections are not directly comparable.

relius_title: INSTRUCTIONS TO ADMINISTRATOR AND NOTIFICATION TO PARTICIPANTS
relius_provision_text: For Plan Years beginning after the end of the Plan Year in which this document is first adopted, if a “Flexible Discretionary Match” contribution formula applies (i.e., a formula that provides an Employer with discretion regarding how to allocate a matching contribution to Participants) and the Employer makes a “Flexible Discretionary Match” to the Plan, the Employer must provide the Plan Administrator or Trustee written instructions describing (1) how the “Flexible Discretionary Match” formula will be allocated to Participants (e.g., a uniform percentage of Elective Deferrals or a flat dollar amount), (2) the computation period(s) to which the “Flexible Discretionary Match” formula applies, and (3) if applicable, a description of each business location or business classification subject to separate “Flexible Discretionary Match” allocation formulas. Such instructions must be provided no later than the date on which the “Flexible Discretionary Match” is made to the Plan. A summary of these instructions must be communicated to Participants. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means Matching Contributions made pursuant to the Safe Harbor CODA formula described in Adoption Agreement Section Three, in an amount equal to (i) 100 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that do not exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation for the Plan Year, plus (ii) 50 percent of the amount of the Employee's Elective Deferrals that exceed three-percent of the Employee's Compensation but that do not exceed five-percent of the Employee's Compensation, if applicable. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to matching contributions, but they govern different types of matching contributions. The Relius provision describes a 'Flexible Discretionary Match' with employer discretion, while the Ascensus provision outlines a specific Safe Harbor matching formula. The options available in each are not directly comparable as they pertain to different matching structures.
variance_notes: The Relius provision allows for discretionary matching contributions with specific instructions required, while the Ascensus provision specifies a defined matching formula based on employee deferrals. This fundamental difference in structure affects the plan outcomes.

relius_provision_text: Each Participating Employer shall be deemed to be a part of this Plan; provided, however, that with respect to all its relations with the Trustee (or Insurer) and Administrator for purposes of this Plan, each Participating Employer shall be deemed to have designated irrevocably the Employer as its agent. Unless the context of the Plan clearly indicates otherwise, the word "Employer" shall be deemed to include each Participating Employer as related to its adoption of the Plan. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: If the Plan is a money purchase pension plan, Employer Prevailing Wage Contributions will be designated as Employer Money Purchase Pension Plan Contributions. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions relate to employer contributions, but they govern different types of contributions and do not provide equivalent options or elections. The Relius provision discusses the designation of the employer as an agent for various types of contributions, while the Ascensus provision specifically addresses money purchase pension plan contributions and does not mention the broader context of employer designations or the same range of contribution types.
variance_notes: The Relius provision includes a broader scope of employer contributions (including profit sharing and prevailing wage contributions) and emphasizes the agency relationship, while the Ascensus provision is limited to money purchase pension plan contributions without addressing agency or other contribution types.

relius_provision_text: Paid during "determination period." Compensation shall include only that Compensation which is actually paid to the Participant during the "determination period." Except as otherwise provided in this Plan, the "determination period" is the period elected by the Employer in the Adoption Agreement. If the Employer makes no election, the "determination period" shall be the Plan Year. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
ascensus_provision_text: Means, except as provided elsewhere in this Plan, the Plan Year unless the Adopting Employer has selected another period in the Adoption Agreement. [Linked to 5 AA election(s)]
reasoning: Both provisions define the 'determination period' for contributions, indicating it is typically the Plan Year unless an alternative is selected in the Adoption Agreement. They govern the same plan feature regarding compensation during the determination period.
